agents.description: The council's only source for textual evidence. Transcribes everything visible — road signs, shopfronts, graffiti, vehicle text — and maps scripts and languages to candidate countries. Distinguishes near-identical scripts (Cyrillic variants, CJK forms) and notes when a language is shared across multiple countries.

Reads physical geography from the image: terrain morphology, vegetation density, soil colour, sun angle, road texture, and settlement layout. Narrows down the climate zone and broad geographic region before any country-level reasoning happens.

Identifies plants visible in the image — trees, undergrowth, crops, and ornamentals — and uses biogeography to constrain the country. Recognises endemic species and crops that pin down regions with high precision (e.g. olive cultivation, eucalyptus monoculture, betel-nut palms).

Studies road infrastructure: driving side, road markings, signage shapes and colours, license plate formats, traffic lights, guard rails, kilometre posts. These are the highest-signal eliminators in GeoGuessr — a single visible plate or sign can collapse the search to one country.

The GeoGuessr-meta specialist. Recognises bollards, utility poles, guardrails, mailboxes, road paint quality, the Google Street View camera's own metadata (rig generation, blur style), and any object whose design is country-specific without being intentional signage.

collaborationSteps.title: Specialist Assessment
collaborationSteps.description: All five specialists analyse the same Google Street View image in parallel. Each agent only uses its own expertise — text, terrain, flora, infrastructure, or meta — and proposes candidate countries with confidence levels.

collaborationSteps.title: Region Consensus Check
collaborationSteps.description: A judge agent maps every candidate to a world region (Europe, East Asia, etc.). If all five specialists agree on the same region, the council fast-tracks to country-level reasoning (Path A). If not, it enters region hypothesis evaluation (Path B).

collaborationSteps.title: Region Hypothesis Evaluation
collaborationSteps.description: Path B only. The judge generates 2–4 region hypotheses and each specialist evaluates them in isolation on a 5-level scale (strongly supports → strongly contradicts). No anchoring between regions. The judge picks the winning region.

collaborationSteps.title: Region-Bound Assessment
collaborationSteps.description: Path B only. Once the region is locked, specialists re-assess the image with that region as a hard constraint, proposing only countries within it. The judge collects every country named by ≥3 agents plus the top candidates into a country-hypothesis set.

collaborationSteps.title: Country Hypothesis Evaluation
collaborationSteps.description: Each specialist scores every country hypothesis on the 5-level confidence scale. Hypotheses are shuffled per agent so positional bias doesn't correlate across the council. The judge aggregates the votes with hard-eliminator rules.

collaborationSteps.title: Country Determination
collaborationSteps.description: The judge weighs the country evaluations, resolves any remaining ties, and outputs the final country plus a coordinate estimate with a written rationale.
